Two-panel residual diagnostic plot

Source: R/plot.R
dot-plot_residuals.Rd

Constructs two diagnostic panels using existing S3 methods:

  1. Residuals vs fitted: Pearson residuals (vertical) against fitted values \(\hat{q}_i \hat\mu_i\) (horizontal). Under correct model specification, the residuals should scatter randomly around zero with no systematic pattern. A LOESS smoother (red) is overlaid to highlight trends.

  2. Normal QQ plot: Sorted Pearson residuals against theoretical standard normal quantiles \(\Phi^{-1}((i - 0.375)/(N + 0.25))\). Departures from the diagonal indicate non-normality of the residual distribution.

Usage

.plot_residuals(object)

Arguments

object

An hbb_fit object.

Value

A patchwork object combining two ggplot panels.

Details

The panels are combined via patchwork.

Pearson residuals

The Pearson residual for observation \(i\) is $$ r_i^P = \frac{y_i - n_i \hat{q}_i \hat\mu_i} {\sqrt{\widehat{\mathrm{Var}}(Y_i)}}, $$ where \(\widehat{\mathrm{Var}}(Y_i)\) is the hurdle variance computed from \((n_i, \hat{q}_i, \hat\mu_i, \hat\kappa)\). See hurdle_variance for the variance decomposition.
